column1:
What 2D artifact is demonstrated on the image?

A: edge shadowing/defocusing
B: edge enhancement
C: side lobes
D: mirror image
column2:
Answer: A. edge shadowing/defocusing

Feedback:
Caused by a reduction of reflection echoes by diffraction of the sound waves that touch the margin of a round boundary.
Commonly seen in transverse views of blood vessels.

column1:
What is the Framingham risk score?

A: criteria used to determine appropriate use of physiologic testing
B: gender specific criteria for estimation of 10 year risk of cardiovascular disease
C: criteria used to determine appropriate use of vascular ultrasound
D: gender specific criteria for estimation of 10 year risk of pulmonary embolism
column2:
Answer: B. gender specific criteria for estimation of 10 year risk of cardiovascular disease

Feedback:
Framingham risk score - gender specific criteria for estimation of 10 year risk of cardiovascular disease. Low scores indicate
screening exams are unnecessary . High scores may be appropriate for screening in some cases.

column1:
Which of the following is the most likely explanation for findings displayed on the image?

A: The superficial vessel is a mirror image artifact caused by the apex of the lung
B: The subclavian artery is duplicated and both vessels demonstrate normal flow
C: The deeper vessel is a mirror image artifact caused by the apex of the lung
D: The normal subclavian artery is displayed with significant reflux demonstrated in the subclavian vein
column2:
Answer: C. The deeper vessel is a mirror image artifact caused by the apex of the lung

Feedback:
The deeper vessel is a mirror image artifact caused by the apex of the lung. The lung apex is located immediately posterior to
the subclavian artery and acts as a highly reflective acoustic interface that causes the mirror image artifact. Mirror image
artifacts cause the incorrect display of a duplicate structure deep to the actual structure. The subclavian artery could possibly be
duplicated but it is very rare.

column1:
Which of the following statements best describes the reason why there is an absence of color flow in the vessel on the image?

A: The color Doppler gain is set too low to detect the slower flow at the curve in the vessel.
B: There is an occlusion present. Flow is demonstrated proximal to the lesion with collateral flow filling the distal portion of the vessel.
C: The ultrasound beam is perpendicular to the motion of the blood so no flow is detected.
D: The ultrasound beam is parallel to the motion of the blood so no flow is detected.
column2:
Answer: C. The ultrasound beam is perpendicular to the motion of the blood so no flow is detected.

Feedback:
2D imaging relies in perpendicular incidence of the beam for the best image. Doppler evaluation detects blood moving parallel
to the axis of the beam. If flow is moving perpendicular to the beam (90 degrees), no flow will be detected.

column1:
The brightness of the reflection on the Doppler tracing represents __________________.

A: the number of red blood cells moving at each velocity displayed
B: the time duration of the reflection
C: the spatial pulse length of the reflection
D: the frequency of the reflection
column2:
Answer: A. the number of red blood cells moving at each velocity displayed

Feedback:
The brightness of the reflection on the Doppler tracing represents the number of red blood cells moving at each velocity
displayed. It is directly related to the signal strength.

column1:
Which of the following is a disadvantage of magnetic resonance angiography compared to Doppler evaluation of the carotid arteries?

A: Contrast is always required for patients with a history of renal disease.
B: MRA of the carotid arteries can only be performed on patients who are not diabetic.
C: Patients on anticoagulant therapy cannot undergo an MRA evaluation.
D: MRA exams tend to overestimate the level of stenosis present.
column2:
Answer: D. MRA exams tend to overestimate the level of stenosis present.

Feedback:
MRA exams tend to overestimate the level of stenosis present when compared to Doppler findings. Contrast may be used on
the exam but is not always required. Certain patients are at a higher risk of renal failure from the contrast.

column1:
If a renal artery Doppler exam demonstrates normal flow and normal flow is identified on angiography evaluation, the US results are described as:

A: false positive
B: true negative
C: false negative
D: true positive
column2:
Answer: B. true negative

Feedback:
If the current exam is negative but the gold standard is positive = false negative results on current exam
If the current exam is positive but the gold standard is negative= false positive results on current exam
If the current exam is negative and the gold standard is negative = true negative results on current exam
If the current exam is positive and the gold standard is positive = true positive results on current exam

column1:
Abdominal Doppler exams are usually best performed with a frequency of _________MHz and Lower Extremity Arterial Duplex exams are usually best performed with a frequency of _____MHz.

A: 2-4MHz, 5-7MHz
B: 5-7MHz, 8-10MHz
C: 1-3MHz, 5-7MHz
D: 2-4MHz, 8-10MHz
column2:
Answer: A. 2-4MHz, 5-7MHz

Feedback:
Abdominal Doppler exams are usually best performed with a frequency of 2-4MHz and Lower Extremity Arterial Duplex exams
are usually best performed with a frequency of 5-7MHz.

column1:
According to the Appropriate Use Criteria, which of the following is an inappropriate indication for an aortic duplex exam?

A: 1 month follow up after endograft placement
B: Lower extremity claudication
C: Pulsatile abdominal mass
D: Fever of unknown origin
column2:
Answer: D. Fever of unknown origin

Feedback:
Isolated findings of a fever of unknown origin is not an appropriate indication for any vascular ultrasound exam. It is a non-
specific symptom that has rarely been associated with vascular disease. Unilateral lower extremity symptoms can be caused by
iliac artery disease and bilateral symptoms can be related to aortic disease. A pulsatile abdominal mass is a clinical indication of
AAA. If endograft placement is performed to treat an AAA, the baseline exam should be performed within 1 month of the
procedure.

column1:
If new research shows that the minimum velocity criteria used to diagnose >60% renal artery stenosis is actually 225cm/s, not 180cm/s, how will this affect the negative predictive value of the exam?

A: no effect on the negative predictive value, only affects accuracy
B: increase by 4.5%
C: decrease by 4.5%
D: decrease will vary with patient population
column2:
Answer: D. decrease will vary with patient population

Feedback:
Negative predictive value refers to the ability of an exam to correctly predict the absence of disease. Raising the peak velocity
required to identify a significant renal stenosis will decrease the negative predictive value. This occurs due to the increased
amount of patients that have a lower velocity but truly demonstrate >60% stenosis in the renal artery.
The positive predictive value will increase for the renal Doppler exam because more patients will actually have significant
stenosis when their exam produces velocities above 225cm/s.

column1:
Which of the following explains why a patient can receive an US diagnosis of 70% ICA stenosis, but an angiography diagnosis of 50% ICA stenosis?

A: There was most likely a difference in the patient's cardiac output on the dates the two exams were performed.
B: Angiography calculates the area stenosis and the US exam offers the diameter stenosis.
C: Angiography only evaluates the longitudinal axis of vessel which can lead to over- or under-estimation of the stenosis.
D: The patient has severe hyperlipidemia with acute disease progression between the two exams.
column2:
Answer: C. Angiography only evaluates the longitudinal axis of vessel which can lead to over- or under-estimation of the stenosis.

Feedback:
Angiography only evaluates the longitudinal axis of vessel, which can lead to over- or under-estimation of the stenosis. Only the
diameter stenosis can be estimated with this method. If the vessel is curved or tortuous, it can make estimation of stenosis
difficult on a longitudinal 2-dimensional angio image.

column1:
Which of the following indicates the proper order for putting on personal protective equipment?

A: goggles, gown, mask, gloves
B: gloves, gown, mask, goggles
C: gown, mask, goggles, gloves
D: mask, goggles, gloves, gown
column2:
Answer: C. gown, mask, goggles, gloves

Feedback:
Before putting on PPE, always wash your hands first. When putting on PPE, the CDC recommends putting the gown on first,
then the mask, followed by goggles and finish with the gloves.

column1:
What is indicated by the red arrows on this subcostal image?

A: Abdominal Aorta
B: Portal Vein
C: IVC
D: Mirror image artifact
column2:
Answer: D. Mirror image artifact

Feedback:
The connective tissue is very reflective which causes a mirror image of the heart, IVC and liver tissue.

column1:
Hyperostosis is a limitation of which exam?

A: transcranial Doppler exam
B: renal artery Doppler exam
C: lower extremity venous Doppler exam
D: lower extremity arterial Doppler exam
column2:
Answer: A. transcranial Doppler exam

Feedback:
Hyperostosis refers to the thickening of bones that is most commonly seen in women and older patients. The thicker bones
limit sound penetration during a transcranial duplex exam.

column1:
If new research shows that the minimum velocity criteria used to diagnose >60% renal artery stenosis is actually 225cm/s, not 180cm/s, how will this affect the accuracy of the renal Doppler exam?

A: increase by 4.5%
B: increase will vary with patient population
C: decrease will vary with patient population
D: no effect on the accuracy, only affects positive and negative predictive value
column2:
Answer: B. increase will vary with patient population

Feedback:
Negative predictive value refers to the ability of an exam to correctly predict the absence of disease. Raising the peak velocity
required to identify a significant renal stenosis will decrease the negative predictive value. This occurs due to the increased
amount of patients that have a lower velocity but truly demonstrate >60% stenosis in the renal artery.
The positive predictive value will increase for the renal Doppler exam because more patients will actually have significant
stenosis when their exam produces velocities above 225cm/s.
Accuracy will increase because of the increase in truly positive exams compared to the total number of exams. The more people
correctly diagnosed as positive, the greater the accuracy of an exam.

column1:
When considering the effectiveness of the carotid Doppler exam at your lab, the negative predictive value is 88%, the positive predictive value is 95%, the sensitivity is 85% and the specificity is 90%. Using those parameters, which of the following could be the value for the accuracy of the testing technique?

A: 89%
B: 92%
C: 96%
D: 84%
column2:
Answer: A. 89%

Feedback:
The accuracy of a technique falls between the NPV and PPV AND between the sensitivity and specificity. 89% is higher than
88%NPV and lower than 95%PPV; 89% is also higher than 85% sensitivity and lower than 90% specificity.

column1:
How do you adjust your technique to reduce or eliminate cross talk on a Doppler tracing?

A: Use the same approach and incident angle but use a CW non-imaging probe
B: Increase the PRF
C: Turn down the Doppler gain
D: Use a smaller sample size
column2:
Answer: C. Turn down the Doppler gain

Feedback:
The mirror image artifact on Doppler is caused by sampling close to a very reflective structure (bone) and by using a Doppler
angle near 90 degrees. If you use the same approach but switch to CW, the same issue will occur. To fix the issue, try a new
window that offers more space between the reflective structure and the artery, turn down the Doppler gain, or try a new
window where the vessel course is less perpendicular to the probe.

column1:
Which of the following correctly describes the specificity of a testing technique?

A: If f the test result is negative specificity is the probability that the patient does not have disease
B: The ability of a test to detect disease when it IS present.
C: The ability of a test to rule out disease when it IS NOT present.
D: If the test result is positive, specificity is the probability that the patient actually has the disease
column2:
Answer: C. The ability of a test to rule out disease when it IS NOT present.

Feedback:
Specificity - The ability of a test to rule out disease when it IS NOT present. The number of truly negative tests performed in the
current modality is compared to the negative tests diagnosed by the gold standard test

column1:
Which type of image resolution makes it possible to differentiate the intimal media thickness from the outer arterial wall?

A: lateral
B: temporal
C: axial
D: elevational
column2:
Answer: C. axial

Feedback:
Axial resolution refers to the machine's ability to resolve structures that are parallel to the ultrasound beam. Measuring the
anterior-posterior thickness of a structure, such as the wall thickness of an artery or the thickness of the gallbladder wall, relies
on the axial resolution of the system.

column1:
______________________________ is defined as the time from onset of systole to the time peak systole occurs.

A: acceleration time
B: velocity time interval
C: spatial pulse length
D: resistive index
column2:
Answer: A. acceleration time

Feedback:
Acceleration time is defined as the time from onset of systole to the time peak systole occurs. It decreases as resistance
increases. Increased resistance causes a short time between the onset of systole and peak systole.

column1:
When removing a protective gown:

A: untie the strings, roll the gown upward from the bottom edge to turn it inside out, pull it from the shoulders and off the arms, remove your gloves, wash your hands
B: untie the strings, pull the gown forward from the shoulders using the neck strings to turn it inside out as it is removed, remove your gloves, wash your hands
C: remove your gloves, untie the strings, pull the gown forward from the shoulders using the neck strings, grab the inside shoulder seams to pull it off the arms and turn it inside out as it is removed, wash your hands
D: remove your gloves, untie the strings, pull the gown forward from the center of the front of the gown to remove it with the exposed side out, roll it into a ball, wash your hands
column2:
Answer: C. remove your gloves, untie the strings, pull the gown forward from the shoulders using the neck strings, grab the inside shoulder seams to pull it off the arms and turn it inside out as it is removed, wash your hands

Feedback:
When removing a protective gown, remove your gloves, untie the strings, pull the gown forward from the shoulders using the
neck strings, grab the inside shoulder seams pull it off the arms and turn it inside out as it is removed, wash your hands.

column1:
Which statement is true about the image?

A: The red arrows are pointing to the anterior wall of the Baker's cyst and the associated enhancement.
B: The red arrows are pointing to the posterior wall of the Baker's cyst and the associated shadowing.
C: The red arrows are pointing to the posterior wall of the Baker's cyst and the associated enhancement.
D: The red arrows are pointing to the anterior wall of the Baker's cyst and the associated shadowing.
column2:
Answer: A. The red arrows are pointing to the anterior wall of the Baker's cyst and the associated enhancement.

Feedback:
The popliteal fossa is evaluated with a posterior transducer approach. The posterior border of the Baker's cyst is demonstrated
most anterior on the image. The anterior border of the Baker's cyst is demonstrated posteriorly on the image with increased
posterior enhancement.

column1:
What adjustments can be made to improve the attached image of the Doppler tracing to make it of diagnostic quality?

A: Decrease velocity scale
B: Increase the sample volume size
C: Lower the baseline
D: Increase the sweep speed
column2:
Answer: C. Lower the baseline

Feedback:
Increase scale, Lower freq. probe, Change angle°, Adjust approach to decrease distance to vessel, Switch to CW.

column1:
A thrill is a normal clinical finding with _______________________, but an abnormal clinical finding with___________________________.

A: recent iliac stent placement, a hemodialysis graft
B: a hemodialysis fistula, recent iliac stent placement
C: lower extremity vein graft, lower extremity prosthetic graft
D: thoracic outlet syndrome, Raynaud syndrome
column2:
Answer: B. a hemodialysis fistula, recent iliac stent placement

Feedback:
A thrill is a normal clinical finding with hemodialysis AVF, but an abnormal clinical finding after any invasive procedure of the
lower extremity.

column1:
Which setting should be lowered to the minimum level when beginning to evaluate the patient using the orbital window?

A: Output power
B: Wall filter
C: Velocity scale
D: Doppler gain
column2:
Answer: A. Output power

Feedback:
The intensity of the sound beam passed through the eye should be set to the lowest level possible.

column1:
Which of the following correctly describes a surveillance exam?

A: testing used to monitor known disease progression
B: testing used to determine the presence or absence of disease in an asymptomatic patient
C: the initial exam following vascular intervention/surgery that is usually done within first month after the procedure
D: testing that leads to the initial diagnosis
column2:
Answer: A. testing used to monitor known disease progression

Feedback:
Screening examination: testing used to determine the presence or absence of disease in an asymptomatic patient
Baseline examination: testing that leads to the initial diagnosis or the initial exam following vascular intervention/surgery that is
usually done within first month after the procedure
Surveillance examination: testing used to monitor known disease progression; follow up exam timing is based on the time since
initial diagnosis or revascularization

column1:
If the Doppler cursor angle is underestimated, the velocity of the flow will be ____________________.

A: be unaffected
B: dependent solely on the strength of the frequency shift
C: underestimated
D: overestimated
column2:
Answer: C. underestimated

Feedback:
The Doppler angle of incidence is the angle at which the Doppler beam intersects the blood flow. The cursor angle is aligned
with the vessel walls. If this angle is underestimated, the velocity of the flow will be underestimated. If this angle is
overestimated, the velocity of flow will be overestimated.

column1:
If a renal artery Doppler exam demonstrates stenosis but normal flow is identified on angiography evaluation, the US results are described as:

A: true positive
B: false positive
C: false negative
D: true negative
column2:
Answer: B. false positive

Feedback:
If the current exam is negative but the gold standard is positive = false negative results on current exam
If the current exam is positive but the gold standard is negative= false positive results on current exam
If the current exam is negative and the gold standard is negative = true negative results on current exam
If the current exam is positive and the gold standard is positive = true positive results on current exam

column1:
How can a Sonographer correct for blooming/blossoming on a spectral Doppler display?

A: reduce the wall filter
B: turn the Doppler gain down
C: decrease the color Doppler velocity scale
D: increase the color Doppler velocity scale
column2:
Answer: B. turn the Doppler gain down

Feedback:
Blooming or blossoming describes artifactual echoes displayed around a waveform shape. It is caused by over-gaining and can
lead to overestimation of the peak velocity. Properly adjusted gain will demonstrate a waveform of medium brightness and a
clear spectral window(if present).

column1:
The best obtainable angle of insonation for vascular evaluation that is most commonly used to provide Doppler shift information is_________degrees.

A: 0
B: 45
C: 60
D: 90
column2:
Answer: C. 60

Feedback:
The best angle of insonation to produce the most accurate Doppler shift information is 0 degrees. The best OBTAINABLE angle
of insonation of a blood vessel that is most commonly used to provide Doppler shift information is 60 degrees.

column1:
Which of the following correctly describes the color displayed on the image?

A: The heel-to-toe method can be used to change the course of the vessel across the image and this will allow the vessel to accurately display a single color
B: The color scale should be reduced so that the entire vessel is filled with blue color.
C: The color scale should be reduced so that the entire vessel is filled with red color.
D: The red and blue colors indicate that flow is moving in two different directions simultaneously in the vessel
column2:
Answer: A. The heel-to-toe method can be used to change the course of the vessel across the image and this will allow the vessel to accurately display a single color

Feedback:
Color is displayed according to the direction the flow is moving in relation to the ultrasound beam. If the flow is perpendicular to
the beam, no color will be displayed. This color map indicates flow toward the transducer is red and away is blue. To improve
the color display in the image, the transducer should be angled (heel-to - toe) to demonstrate the vessel at a steeper angle
across the screen. This will eliminate the area without color display that is perpendicular to the beam. It will also allow all flow in
the vessel to be displayed as moving in the same direction.

column1:
The recommended sample size for PW Doppler evaluation of an artery is?

A: 0.5mm
B: 3-4mm
C: 0.5cm
D: 1-1.5mm
column2:
Answer: D. 1-1.5mm

Feedback:
Small sample sizes demonstrate a more specific location of the velocity detected.

column1:
What system setting would result in false-positive findings for reflux in the great saphenous vein?

A: high color persistence settings
B: high velocity scale settings
C: high wall filter settings
D: high frame rate
column2:
Answer: A. high color persistence settings

Feedback:
High color persistence settings may result in false-positive color-flow findings for reflux. Increasing color persistence increases
the amount of time the color is displayed on the image as blood flows. Increasing the color persistence can give the illusion of
significant reflux because the color display is prolonged.
High filter and velocity scale settings would result in less color display. A higher frame rate indicates a higher screen refresh
rate and no extension of display of the color.

column1:
When wearing a protective gown and gloves:

A: the open end of the gloves should be fitted under the wrist cuffs of the gown
B: the open end of the gloves should be pulled over the wrist cuffs of the gown
C: two pairs of gloves should be worn
D: the open end of the gloves should not be touching the wrist cuffs of the gown
column2:
Answer: B. the open end of the gloves should be pulled over the wrist cuffs of the gown

Feedback:
When wearing a protective gown and gloves the open end of the gloves should be pulled over the wrist cuffs of the gown. The
hands and arms are then fully protected.

column1:
Which of the following Doppler information is necessary to calculate the resistive index?

A: peak systolic velocity, average flow velocity and end diastolic velocity
B: peak systolic velocity and peak diastolic velocity
C: peak systolic velocity and end diastolic velocity
D: peak systolic velocity before and after a stenosis
column2:
Answer: C. peak systolic velocity and end diastolic velocity

Feedback:
The resistive index = PSV - EDV / PSV

column1:
Which of the following will maintain compliance with the ALARA principle?

A: reducing overall gain
B: reducing the number of images taken in standard protocol
C: reducing transducer frequency
D: reducing the amount of protected information given to the patient
column2:
Answer: B. reducing the number of images taken in standard protocol

Feedback:
As Low As Reasonably Achievable refers the exposure to ultrasound. Minimizing the number of standard images taken on each
patient will reduce the exam time and exposure. Reducing frequency and gain do not affect the intensity/exposure time.

column1:
What should be done to adjust and improve the color displayed on the attached image?

A: Decrease color baseline
B: Steer color box
C: Increase PRF
D: Increase velocity scale
column2:
Answer: B. Steer color box

Feedback:
The goal for color Doppler processing is to demonstrate blood that is moving in a direction parallel to the US beam. Vessels
displayed in longitudinal plane are more perpendicular to the beam. By angling the probe and the color box you cause the flow
to be more parallel to the beam. Color Doppler display improves when the flow is more parallel in relationship to the beam.

column1:
What is the gold standard method of diagnosing temporal arteritis?

A: duplex ultrasound
B: biopsy
C: CTA
D: MRA
column2:
Answer: B. biopsy

Feedback:
Biopsy of the temporal artery is the gold standard method of diagnosis for temporal arteritis.

column1:
What can be done to improve the visualization of the anterior aortic wall?

A: scan from a more lateral approach
B: center the probe with the ultrasound beam perpendicular to the aortic wall
C: increase the compression
D: decrease the threshold setting.
column2:
Answer: B. center the probe with the ultrasound beam perpendicular to the aortic wall

Feedback:
Perpendicular incidence of the beam to the reflector will increase specular reflection. Increasing the amount of specular
reflection will improve the visibility of the anterior aortic wall. 2D imaging relies on perpendicular incidence of the beam to the
structure while Doppler imaging relies on parallel incidence of the beam to the blood flow.

column1:
According to the Appropriate Use Criteria, which of the following is NOT a valid exam indication for a duplex exam on a hemodialysis fistula?

A: Inability to cannulate at dialysis
B: palpable thrill at the fistula site
C: 1 month follow up baseline exam post-intervention
D: ipsilateral arm swelling
column2:
Answer: B. palpable thrill at the fistula site

Feedback:
Hemodialysis fistulas and grafts will normally demonstrate a thrill over the connection site in the arm. Absence of the thrill can
indicate an occlusion of the fistula/graft.

column1:
When a clot is present on a venogram, how does it appear on the image?

A: "empty" vein with no contrast opacification
B: dark solid area within the vein
C: similar to a calcification, very echogenic
D: complex mass obstructing contrast flow
column2:
Answer: A. "empty" vein with no contrast opacification

Feedback:
Iodinated contrast is injected into the venous system at the foot. The contrast will fill any vein that is patent. Thrombus will
prevent the vein from filling with contrast.

column1:
Which of the following requires a Doppler cursor angle of 60 degrees or less for accurate reporting of numerical values?

A: peak systolic velocity
B: resistive index
C: pulsatility index
D: S/D ratio
column2:
Answer: A. peak systolic velocity

Feedback:
The resistive index, pulsatility index and S/D ratios are not dependent on the Doppler cursor angle for accurate calculations.
Because the values reported are ratios and not velocities, Doppler angle is not relevant to the results of the calculations. When
the peak systolic or diastolic velocities of flow are reported, the Doppler cursor angle must be used to calculate and report
accurate values.

column1:
What can be done to reduce the spectral broadening on the image?

A: decrease rejection
B: decrease Doppler Gain
C: decrease the PRF
D: decrease wall filter
column2:
Answer: B. decrease Doppler Gain

Feedback:
The image is significantly over-gained. The other choices would only increase the appearance of low frequency reflections/noise.

column1:
According to the Appropriate Use Criteria, which of the following is an appropriate indication for an renal artery duplex exam?

A: Bilateral renal enlargement
B: Unilateral renal enlargement
C: Hypertension in a young person (age <35 years)
D: Peripheral arterial disease with well controlled hypertension
column2:
Answer: C. Hypertension in a young person (age <35 years)

Feedback:
Uncontrollable or excessively high blood pressure (>160/80mmHg) are common indications for a renal artery duplex because
the kidneys can raise the BP is their arterial flow is limited by stenosis. Hypertension in younger patients can be a sign of RAS,
usually due to fibromuscular dysplasia. Discrepancy in kidney size can be a sign of RAS. Chronic stenosis can cause atrophy of
the kidney that has limited arterial inflow. RAS can be the cause for kidneys that differ in length by more than 2cm .
***The appropriate use criteria offers common sense reasons for ordering exams. When evaluating these questions, consider
the effectiveness of the exam in providing a diagnosis. We do not recommend that you attempt to memorize all indications.

column1:
Which of the following exams is typically performed without angle correction on Doppler evaluation?

A: TIPS evaluation
B: aorta duplex
C: IVC duplex
D: renal artery duplex
column2:
Answer: C. IVC duplex

Feedback:
Any Doppler exam that requires measurement of blood flow velocity requires angle correction to obtain an accurate velocity.
This includes all arterial exams and TIPS evaluations. A duplex exam of the IVC is used to assess patency and flow variation with
respiration and cardiac pulsatility. No velocity information is used in the IVC duplex exam.

column1:
What system control can reduce the appearance of clutter on a color Doppler image?

A: decreasing color scale
B: increasing color threshold
C: increasing persistence
D: decreasing color priority
column2:
Answer: D. decreasing color priority

Feedback:
Reducing color on an image can be done by decreasing color gain, increasing the PRF/color scale, reducing color persistence,
increasing rejection, increasing wall filter, reducing color threshold and reducing color priority.
Color Priority: Adjusts the threshold setting; Determines if a pixel is displayed as shade of gray, black or white or as a color;
Lower threshold settings display less color (lower color priority); Higher threshold settings display more color (higher color
priority); Necessary to minimize color flash(clutter) and improper placement of color on vessel walls

column1:
What console control should be adjusted to improve the quality of the Doppler tracing?

A: increase the PW Doppler scale
B: adjust the placement of the Doppler cursor so it is more parallel to the blood flow
C: reduce the wall filter setting
D: raise the baseline
column2:
Answer: B. adjust the placement of the Doppler cursor so it is more parallel to the blood flow

Feedback:
This image can be improved in multiple ways. The Doppler cursor is nearly perpendicular to the flow in the vein. By moving the
Doppler cursor to the portion of the vessel on the left side of the image, the flow would be more parallel to the sound beam.
Steering the color box and cursor would also improve the angle of insonation. The highest quality and most accurate Doppler
tracing will be produced when the Doppler cursor is parallel to the flow.
The PW scale could be reduced to help the waveform fill the Doppler area and improve the display of the low flow velocities. The
wall filter could be increased to remove the low level arterial/artifactual Doppler information that appears on the top of the
baseline.

column1:
Pulsatility Index can be used to describe?

A: the amount of blood vessel recoil
B: the pressure gradient within a vessel
C: the amount of variation in the velocity during the cardiac cycle
D: amount of turbulence within a vessel
column2:
Answer: C. the amount of variation in the velocity during the cardiac cycle

Feedback:
The pulsatility index (PI) can be used to describe the amount of variation in the velocity during the cardiac cycle. High resistance
flow demonstrates higher PI values than low resistance flow.

column1:
What is the cause of the vertical acoustic shadow seen on the images?

A: electrical interference
B: element damage
C: air in the trachea
D: patient motion
column2:
Answer: B. element damage

Feedback:
Note the vertical black stripe that originates at the face of the transducer. It is located in the same place on the image as you
scan different anatomy.

column1:
Which of the following describes the correct way to measure the acceleration time for a renal artery waveform?

A: place a caliper at the onset of the systolic upstroke and at the early systolic peak
B: place a caliper at the true systolic peak and the end diastolic velocity
C: place a caliper at the early systolic peak and the end diastolic velocity
D: place a caliper at the onset of the systolic upstroke and at the true systolic peak
column2:
Answer: A. place a caliper at the onset of the systolic upstroke and at the early systolic peak

Feedback:
Resistive Index - place a caliper at the true systolic peak and the end diastolic velocity; Acceleration Time - place a caliper at the
onset of the systolic upstroke and at the early systolic peak

column1:
Resistive Index is a ratio utilized to quantify which of the following?

A: phasicity of flow
B: resistance to flow
C: flow velocity
D: turbulence of flow
column2:
Answer: B. resistance to flow

Feedback:
The resistive index compares the systolic and diastolic flow velocities to evaluate tissue resistance to blood flow. Helpful in
evaluation of renal parenchyma, liver parenchyma, and transplant evaluation.

column1: Review the image and the system settings listed along the right side. Use your mouse to position your cursor over the setting that should be adjusted to improve the diagnostic quality of the Doppler tracing. Click to set the cursor and confirm your answer.
column2:
Answer: Unavailable

Feedback:
Doppler settings should always be adjusted to allow the waveform to fill the display area. This allows for proper display and
measurement of waveform characteristics.

column1:
When you change the angle on the Doppler cursor, the ________________ also changes.

A: flow velocity displayed
B: amplitude of the reflected signal
C: the volume of flow interrogated
D: speed of sound in tissue
column2:
Answer: A. flow velocity displayed

Feedback:
Overestimating the angle between the beam and the blood flow = overestimated flow velocity
Underestimating the angle between the beam and the blood flow = underestimated flow velocity

column1:
The image represents what Doppler artifact?

A: Spectral broadening
B: Laminar flow
C: Aliasing
D: Cross Talk
column2:
Answer: C. Aliasing

Feedback:
The waveform wraps around the baseline with the highest velocities displayed incorrectly on the underside of the baseline. This
issue is corrected by increasing the scale, use a lower freq. probe, change angle°, adjust approach to decrease distance to
vessel, switch to CW.

column1:
When you reduce angle correction to make the incident beam more parallel to the blood flow, how does the peak velocity displayed change?

A: no change unless the patient is hypertensive
B: increase
C: decrease
D: no change unless stenosis is present
column2:
Answer: B. increase

Feedback:
As the angle correction is used to make the beam more parallel to the blood flow, the angle between the beam and the flow
decreases. As the angle between the beam and the flow decreases, the Doppler shift increases (the more aligned the cursor is
to the flow direction, the higher the Doppler shift).

column1:
The Doppler tracing demonstrates electrical noise. How can you reduce or eliminate the artifact?

A: Increase the wall filter settings
B: Decrease wall filter settings
C: Decrease the PRF
D: Extend the cord to move the machine as far from the electrical outlet as possible
column2:
Answer: A. Increase the wall filter settings

Feedback:
Noise can be reduced by decreasing Doppler gain, increasing wall filter settings, or trying another wall outlet.

column1:
Which of the following parameters is displayed as the x-axis on a Doppler tracing?

A: velocity
B: speed
C: motion
D: time
column2:
Answer: D. time

Feedback:
Doppler shift frequencies are displayed on a tracing as a function of time; x axis = time, y axis = Doppler shift

column1:
In order to assess low velocity flow, which of the following operator adjustments should be made?

A: decrease wall filter setting and compression
B: increase scale setting
C: decrease compression
D: decrease the rejection and wall filter setting
column2:
Answer: D. decrease the rejection and wall filter setting

Feedback:
Decreasing the wall filter and rejection settings will increase the amount of low level Doppler shifts displayed on the tracing.
Venous flow is low velocity and demonstrates low level Doppler shifts.

column1:
The patient being evaluated has a known cardiac arrhythmia. How would you adjust the Doppler controls to best display the arrhythmia?

A: increase the PW Doppler scale
B: reduce the sweep speed
C: reduce the wall filter setting
D: increase the sample size
column2:
Answer: B. reduce the sweep speed

Feedback:
When a cardiac arrhythmia is present, the sweep speed should be reduced to demonstrate more cardiac cycles on the tracing.
This will allow you to show a few normal cycles before and after the cycle affected by the arrhythmia.

column1:
Which of the following is a reason for lack of color fill in a transverse view of the distal femoral vein?

A: filter settings too low
B: calcified plaque in the distal femoral artery
C: the presence of reflux
D: scale settings too low
column2:
Answer: B. calcified plaque in the distal femoral artery

Feedback:
Significant calcification in the SFA will cause shadowing and limit the transmission of sound to tissues located deeper in the leg.
The femoral vein lies posterior to the SFA and the artifact could inhibit color flow information in the vein.
Low filter settings will allow the transducer to detect lower velocity signals such as venous flow. Low filter settings would
IMPROVE venous flow visualization. Increasing the filter settings could degrade color fill on the image. Venous flow detection
requires a low velocity scale. If a patient has reflux, the vein should be enlarged and flow easily detected.

column1:
If a renal artery Doppler exam demonstrates stenosis and stenosis is identified on angiography evaluation, the US results are described as:

A: false positive
B: true negative
C: false negative
D: true positive
column2:
Answer: D. true positive

Feedback:
If the current exam is negative but the gold standard is positive = false negative results on current exam
If the current exam is positive but the gold standard is negative= false positive results on current exam
If the current exam is negative and the gold standard is negative = true negative results on current exam
If the current exam is positive and the gold standard is positive = true positive results on current exam

column1:
When evaluating heavily calcified arteries, what system settings should be increased to improve the demonstration of flow in the vessels?

A: color gain and angle of insonation
B: velocity scale and persistence
C: persistence and sensitivity
D: frequency and output power
column2:
Answer: C. persistence and sensitivity

Feedback:
Arterial calcification can limit the 2D and color Doppler evaluation. To evaluate heavily calcified arteries use multiple imaging
planes, increase color Doppler gain, increase sensitivity and persistence levels. These changes in system settings should
increase the amount of color displayed.

column1:
Which of the following is an appropriate indication for an extracranial arterial duplex exam?

A: 3 month follow up to evaluate a carotid stent in stable patient
B: Screening exam on a patient with low Framingham risk scoring and no other assessment
C: Follow up exam 6 months after initial diagnosis on patient with <50% ICA stenosis
D: Hollenhorst plaque identified on retinal examination
column2:
Answer: D. Hollenhorst plaque identified on retinal examination

Feedback:
Hollenhorst plaque are evidence of arterial emboli and are identified on retinal examination. The carotid arteries should be
evaluated for the source of the plaque.
Screening exams are not recommended for patients with low Framingham risk scoring and no other assessment. They are
assumed to be at low risk of disease.
6 month follow up exams are usually reserved for patients with ICA stenosis that is 70% or greater.
In the first year after placement of a carotid stent, 1-month baseline exam and a 6-month follow up exam are recommended in
stable patients.
Regarding the Appropriate Use Criteria as a whole, the list of what is not appropriate is easier to commit to memory than what
is appropriate. Review both lists below.

column1:
When a patient has multiple indications for an ultrasound exam, which one is used to determine appropriate use of vascular ultrasound testing?

A: if a patient has multiple symptoms, the Appropriate Use Criteria is invalid
B: the indication with the lowest score should be used to determine appropriate use
C: all scores should be added together and divided by the number of indications
D: the indication with the highest score should be used to determine appropriate use
column2:
Answer: D. the indication with the highest score should be used to determine appropriate use

Feedback:
If there is more than one indication for an exam, the indication with the highest score should be used to determine appropriate
use.

column1:
What is the gold standard evaluation technique for renal artery stenosis?

A: MRA
B: Angiography
C: Ultrasound
D: CTA
column2:
Answer: B. Angiography

Feedback:
Angiography is the gold standard evaluation technique for renal artery stenosis

column1:
If the stenosis/blockage reduces the vessel lumen to 1/2 of the normal vessel area, the velocity of flow at the stenosis must _________________to maintain the flow rate

A: increase by 200%
B: double
C: decrease by 50%
D: quadruple
column2:
Answer: B. double

Feedback:
If the stenosis/blockage reduces the lumen to 1/2 of the normal vessel area, the velocity of flow at the stenosis must double to
maintain the flow rate.

column1:
_____________________ is the gold standard in testing for arterial disease.

A: Segmental Pressures with Duplex US
B: Angiography
C: Angioplasty
D: Venography
column2:
Answer: B. Angiography

Feedback:
Arteriography is the most sensitive and specific test for assessment of arterial disease, but many complications reduce its
application. Aside from the issue of radiation exposure, the invasive nature causes increased risk of infection, abscess or
pseudoaneurysm formation. The iodine based contrast is also not tolerated by all patients.

column1:
The Appropriate Use Criteria were developed:

A: to identify physicians and facilities that need further education on ordering procedures
B: to provide guidance for when to use vascular ultrasound as a secondary exam to confirm a diagnosis
C: by a collaboration of insurance companies seeking to streamline covered services
D: while considering vascular ultrasound or physiologic testing by itself as a reasonable exam choice
column2:
Answer: D. while considering vascular ultrasound or physiologic testing by itself as a reasonable exam choice

Feedback:
the Appropriate Use Criteria for vascular ultrasound and physiologic testing was developed through the collaboration of several
vascular ultrasound and medical societies to improve efficiency and effectiveness of patient diagnosis and treatment. The most
common indications given for vascular ultrasound and physiologic testing were scored for appropriate use. Not all exam
indications were evaluated. The indications were scored while considering vascular ultrasound or physiologic testing by itself as
a reasonable exam choice.
Three categories used to classify indications:
7-9 points: Generally appropriate indication
4-6 points: May be appropriate or reasonable indication; situational consideration
1-3 points: Inappropriate or unreasonable indication
If there is more than one indication for an exam, the indication with the highest score should be used to determine appropriate
use

column1:
Which of the following has the greatest effect on resistance to blood flow?

A: Two consecutive areas of 60% stenosis in a single artery
B: A 60% stenosis in a single artery
C: Two different arteries with 60% stenosis supplying the same organ
D: All scenarios above would have the same variation in resistance
column2:
Answer: A. Two consecutive areas of 60% stenosis in a single artery

Feedback:
Stenoses in a series - multiple areas of stenosis in the same vessel will increase the resistance to flow more than a single
stenotic area; velocity and stenosis can be underestimated at the second stenosis
Stenoses in parallel - stenosis in different vessels coursing in the same direction (collaterals); has a lesser effect on resistance to
flow than series stenoses

column1:
Which of the following describes the correct way to measure the resistive index for a renal artery waveform?

A: place a caliper at the onset of the systolic upstroke and at the true systolic peak
B: place a caliper at the early systolic peak and the end diastolic velocity
C: place a caliper at the true systolic peak and the end diastolic velocity
D: place a caliper at the onset of the systolic upstroke and at the early systolic peak
column2:
Answer: C. place a caliper at the true systolic peak and the end diastolic velocity

Feedback:
Resistive Index - place a caliper at the true systolic peak and the end diastolic velocity; Acceleration Time - place a caliper at the
onset of the systolic upstroke and at the early systolic peak

column1:
Which of the following correctly describes the sensitivity of a testing technique?

A: If the test result is negative sensitivity is the probability that the patient does not have disease
B: If the test result is positive, sensitivity is the probability that the patient actually has the disease
C: The ability of a test to detect disease when it IS present.
D: The ability of a test to rule out disease when it IS NOT present.
column2:
Answer: C. The ability of a test to detect disease when it IS present.

Feedback:
Sensitivity-The ability of a test to detect disease when it IS present. The number of truly positive tests performed in the current
modality is compared to the positive tests diagnosed by the gold standard test.

column1:
When venous flow is slow, echogenic particles can be seen near the walls and in the valvular sinus. This phenomenon is called:

A: misregistration artifact
B: Virchow triad
C: spontaneous contrast
D: clutter artifact
column2:
Answer: C. spontaneous contrast

Feedback:
Slow moving red blood cells around the valve leaflets and near the vein walls can be mildly reflective. This phenomenon is called
spontaneous contrast.

column1:
Which of the following is a system adjustment that is made when changing from a lower extremity arterial exam to a lower extremity venous exam on the same patient?

A: change the cursor angle to 30 degrees
B: decrease transducer frequency
C: reverse the color display baseline
D: decrease Doppler scale
column2:
Answer: D. decrease Doppler scale

Feedback:
Venous flow is much lower velocity than arterial flow which requires a lower Doppler scale. The cursor angle does not need to
be used/adjusted for venous flow evaluation because flow velocity is not being recorded.

column1:
What change in system controls can reduce the appearance of color displayed over vessel walls?

A: decreasing color priority
B: increasing color threshold
C: increasing persistence
D: decreasing color PRF
column2:
Answer: A. decreasing color priority

Feedback:
Color Priority adjusts the threshold setting. It determines if a pixel is displayed as shade of gray, black or white or as a color.
Lower threshold settings reduce the display of color (lower color priority); Higher threshold settings display more color (higher
color priority); Necessary to minimize color flash/clutter and improper placement of color on vessel walls.

column1:
If there is no diastolic flow demonstrated on a Doppler tracing, what will the resistive index be?

A: 0.5
B: 0
C: 1.0
D: unable to calculate the RI, not enough information given
column2:
Answer: C. 1.0

Feedback:
The resistive index is calculated using the peak and end diastolic velocities measured on a Doppler tracing.
PSV - EDV / PSV
Example: PSV 50cm/s, EDV 0cm/s; 50 - 0 / 50 = 1.0

column1:
Which of the following Doppler angles would lead to the most significant error in estimation of flow velocity?

A: 25
B: 45
C: 65
D: 80
column2:
Answer: D. 80

Feedback:
The greater the Doppler angle, the greater the amount if error in the calculated velocity.

column1:
The parenchymal resistance ratio is calculated to assess perfusion of which of the following organs?

A: brain
B: kidney
C: liver
D: muscles
column2:
Answer: B. kidney

Feedback:
Parenchymal resistance ratio = EDV / PSV Used to evaluate the parenchymal resistance in a transplant kidney

column1:
Which of the following system control adjustments will increase the amount of color Doppler that is superimposed over the 2D image?

A: decreased threshold and persistence
B: increased wall filter and increased threshold
C: increased threshold and increased persistence
D: decreased persistence and increased wall filter
column2:
Answer: C. increased threshold and increased persistence

Feedback:
Threshold Control: AKA color priority; Sets the limits for where color will be displayed over the 2D image; Decreased threshold =
decreased color priority; Necessary to minimize color ghosting and improper placement of color on vessel walls
Persistence: increasing color persistence will increase the amount of time a color pixel is displayed over an area

column1:
Which of the following Doppler measurements require(s) the waveform to be traced from start of systole to the end of diastole?

A: resistive index
B: pulsatility index
C: S/D ratio
D: acceleration time
column2:
Answer: B. pulsatility index

Feedback:
The pulsatility index compares the peak velocity to the mean velocity throughout one cardiac cycle. In order to obtain the mean
velocity, tracing of the waveform is required. The PI provides information of the resistance of the distal vascular bed.
The acceleration time is the time from the start of systole to the peak systolic velocity. It is measured by placing a single caliper
at the start of the systolic peak and at one the peak. The S/D ratio is the comparison of the peak systolic and end diastolic flow
velocity. It is measured by placing a single caliper at the peak systolic velocity and a single caliper at the end diastolic velocity. It
can be measured by tracing the waveform but it is not "required". The resistive index uses the peak systolic and end diastolic
velocities to assess resistance in a vessel. A single caliper is placed at the peak systolic velocity and one at the end diastolic
velocity.

column1:
Vessel wall and plaque morphology are best evaluated in 2D when the beam is at what incident angle to the vessel?

A: 0 degrees
B: 45 degrees
C: 60 degrees
D: 90 degrees
column2:
Answer: D. 90 degrees

Feedback:
A perpendicular incidence of the beam to the vessel wall will provide the best reflection for the most accurate information
display on the 2D image. Doppler flow information is most accurate when the beam is parallel (0 degrees) to the flow
movement. The best obtainable Doppler angle is 60 degrees.

column1:
Priapism is an indication for what type of exam?

A: upper extremity segmental pressure
B: mesenteric duplex
C: carotid duplex
D: penile duplex
column2:
Answer: D. penile duplex

Feedback:
Priapism is a painful prolonged erection that occurs without sexual stimuli. It can be caused by veno occlusive disease in the
pelvic veins or an AV fistula in the pelvic region

column1:
If the stenosis/blockage reduces the vessel lumen to 1/4 of the normal vessel area, the velocity of flow at the stenosis must _________________to maintain the flow rate

A: double
B: decrease by 25%
C: increase by 400%
D: quadruple
column2:
Answer: D. quadruple

Feedback:
If the stenosis/blockage reduces the lumen to 1/4 of the normal vessel area, the velocity of flow at the stenosis must quadruple
to maintain the flow rate.

column1:
Which of the following correctly describes a screening exam?

A: testing used to monitor known disease progression
B: the initial exam following vascular intervention/surgery that is usually done within first month after the procedure
C: testing used to determine the presence or absence of disease in an asymptomatic patient
D: testing that leads to the initial diagnosis
column2:
Answer: C. testing used to determine the presence or absence of disease in an asymptomatic patient

Feedback:
Screening examination: testing used to determine the presence or absence of disease in an asymptomatic patient
Baseline examination: testing that leads to the initial diagnosis or the initial exam following vascular intervention/surgery that is
usually done within first month after the procedure
Surveillance examination: testing used to monitor known disease progression; follow up exam timing is based on the time since
initial diagnosis or revascularization

column1:
A ventilation quotient scan is used to evaluate a patient with suspected:

A: pulmonary embolism
B: DVT
C: ICA occlusion
D: portal vein thrombosis
column2:
Answer: A. pulmonary embolism

Feedback:
Ventilation Quotient Scan (VQ Scan) is used to evaluate the lung vasculature for filling defects and abnormalities in perfusion. It
is most commonly performed to assess possible pulmonary embolism. A VQ scan can also be used to evaluate regional lung
function in people with advanced pulmonary disease. If the VQ scan demonstrates a possible PE, a venous duplex exam and/or
echo will be ordered to identify the source of the thrombus.

column1:
What causes the artifact demonstrated on the image?

A: lack of perpendicular incidence between the transducer and blood flow
B: evaluating vessels next to adjacent highly reflective structures
C: bidirectional flow in vessel
D: increased filter settings
column2:
Answer: B. evaluating vessels next to adjacent highly reflective structures

Feedback:
Cross talk is a type of mirror imaging artifact. It is caused by adjacent highly reflective structures, receiver gain settings too high
or incident Doppler angle near 90°.

column1:
Which of the following causes an increase in the resistive index(RI) of the distal common carotid artery?

A: Valsalva maneuver
B: aortic valve insufficiency
C: proximal CCA stenosis
D: proximal ICA stenosis
column2:
Answer: D. proximal ICA stenosis

Feedback:
An occlusion of the ICA will lead to increased resistance to the flow in the ipsilateral common carotid artery.

column1:
Which of the following is a correct statement describing the guidelines for wearing a mask?

A: Wash your hands, put on gloves, hold the mask over your mouth by touching the front of the mask. tie the upper strings over your ears toward the top of your head and the bottom strings behind your neck
B: Wash your hands, hold the mask over your mouth by touching the front of the mask. tie the upper strings over your ears toward the top of your head and the bottom strings behind your neck
C: Wash your hands, tie the upper strings over your ears toward the top of your head and the bottom strings behind your neck
D: Wash your hands, put on gloves, tie the upper strings over your ears toward the top of your head and the bottom strings behind your neck
column2:
Answer: C. Wash your hands, tie the upper strings over your ears toward the top of your head and the bottom strings behind your neck

Feedback:
Proper procedure for wearing a mask: Wash your hands, tie the upper strings over your ears toward the top of your head and
the bottom strings behind your neck. If a gown is required, it goes on next, followed by the gloves at the end. When finished,
remove the gloves first, and then untie the bottom strings, followed by the top strings. Do not touch the center part of mask.
Discard the mask properly and wash your hands.

column1:
According to the Appropriate Use Criteria, which of the following is an appropriate indication for an lower extremity venous duplex exam for venous insufficiency?

A: Suspected or diagnosed pulmonary hypertension
B: Telangiectasia
C: Positive D-dimer test
D: Hyperpigmentation
column2:
Answer: D. Hyperpigmentation

Feedback:
Hyperpigmentation of the distal calf occurs with chronic venous insufficiency. The waste products in the venous blood that
pools in the calf are forced into the subcutaneous tissues by osmosis. Telangiectasia refers to dilated venules that appear as
tiny red/purple veins. They are also called spider veins. While these tiny veins can indicate venous stasis, their isolated
appearance has not been associated with insufficiency in the larger superficial veins.

column1:
Anticoagulant therapy is a contraindication for:

A: cold immersion testing
B: a segmental pressure exam
C: a transcranial duplex exam
D: ultrasound guided thrombin injection
column2:
Answer: D. ultrasound guided thrombin injection

Feedback:
Anticoagulant therapy is a contraindication for pseudoaneurysm treatment. The goal of compression therapy and thrombin
injection is to promote thrombus formation. Anticoagulant therapy works to reduce thrombus formation.

column1:
According to the Appropriate Use Criteria, which of the following is an inappropriate indication for an upper extremity arterial duplex exam?

A: 1 month follow up baseline exam post-intervention
B: unilateral arm swelling
C: unilateral cold hand
D: suspected positional arterial obstruction
column2:
Answer: B. unilateral arm swelling

Feedback:
Limb swelling is a common symptom of extremity venous disease, not arterial disease.

column1:
Which artifact is demonstrated on the image?

A: side lobes
B: reverberation
C: cross talk
D: volume averaging
column2:
Answer: C. cross talk

Feedback:
The Doppler tracing displays a mirror image of the waveform on the opposite side of the baseline. This commonly occurs when
sampling vessels adjacent to a highly reflective structure like a bone.

column1:
Which of the following system adjustments would increase the size of the spectral window?

A: changing from a 2mm to 4mm sample size
B: tortuous vessel
C: decreasing rejection
D: changing from a 4mm to 2mm sample size
column2:
Answer: D. changing from a 4mm to 2mm sample size

Feedback:
Smaller sample sizes will evaluate fewer red blood cells and demonstrate a narrower range of frequency shifts. Reducing the
sample size will display a larger spectral window beneath a narrow range of frequency shifts.

column1:
Which of the following should be increased when evaluating venous flow with color Doppler?

A: wall filter
B: output power
C: color priority
D: PRF
column2:
Answer: C. color priority

Feedback:
The threshold (priority) control is used to adjust the appearance of color Doppler. Increased threshold = increased color display.
Increasing the wall filter or the PRF will reduce the appearance of lower velocity flow. Changes to the power output on the
system will increase the beam intensity and penetration.

column1:
According to the Appropriate Use Criteria, which of the following is an appropriate indication for an lower extremity venous duplex exam?

A: Suspected or diagnosed pulmonary embolism
B: Positive D-dimer test
C: > 4 days in the intensive care unit
D: Screening after recent orthopedic surgery
column2:
Answer: A. Suspected or diagnosed pulmonary embolism

Feedback:
A LE venous duplex exam can be helpful in identifying a potential embolic source in a patient with suspected or diagnosed
pulmonary embolism. A positive D-dimer test can indicate DVT, but it can also indicate numerous unrelated abnormalities. The
lack of sensitivity of the D-dimer test makes it inappropriate indication for a LE venous duplex exam. A prolonged stay in ICU,
without other symptoms related to venous disease, is not been strongly associated with DVT formation. While patients with
recent hip or knee surgery do have an increased risk of developing DVT, a venous duplex exam is not recommended as a
screening exam for all post-surgical patients.

column1:
Which two cursors would be used to measure the velocities used to calculate the resistive index for the flow in this renal artery tracing?

A: yellow and red
B: red and blue
C: red and green
D: blue and yellow
column2:
Answer: D. blue and yellow

Feedback:
The AT is measured by placing a cursor at the start of the systolic upstroke and at the early systolic peak (if present). The
velocities used to calculate the RI are measured by placing the cursor at the true peak systolic velocity and the end diastolic
velocity.

column1:
Which of the following describes how to properly set the color Doppler gain levels for an image?

A: increase the gain until aliasing is noted and then decrease to just below that level
B: increase the gain until blossoming is noted and then decrease to just below that level
C: starting at a high setting with aliasing displayed, decrease the gain until only one color is displayed throughout the image
D: starting at a low setting, increase the gain until only one color is displayed throughout the image
column2:
Answer: B. increase the gain until blossoming is noted and then decrease to just below that level

Feedback:
Proper color Doppler gain setting involves increasing the gain until blossoming is noted and then decreasing to just below that
level.
Aliasing is best resolved by increasing the color scale, not gain.

column1:
Which of the following would be the probe frequency of choice for a TCD exam?

A: 2MHz
B: 4MHz
C: 7MHz
D: 10MHz
column2:
Answer: A. 2MHz

Feedback:
TCD requires a low MHz probe to penetrate the skull.

column1:
Which of the following parameters is displayed as the y-axis on a Doppler tracing?

A: motion
B: frequency shift
C: speed
D: time
column2:
Answer: B. frequency shift

Feedback:
Doppler shift frequencies are displayed on a tracing as a function of time; x axis = time, y axis = Doppler shift; Velocity could also
be a correct answer, but speed is incorrect. Speed does not indicate the direction of the motion. Doppler evaluation provides
the speed and direction of the flow = velocity.

column1:
What console controls should be adjusted to improve the image?

A: increase the color Doppler scale
B: increase the PW Doppler scale
C: drop the baseline
D: more than one of the above
column2:
Answer: D. more than one of the above

Feedback:
This image can be improved in multiple ways. There is color bleed outside the vessel on the color image. This indicates that the
color scale is too low and should be increased. The waveform should fill the PW Doppler tracing area. The PW Doppler scale
should be reduced to enlarge the waveform. The retrograde component of the flow is estimated at 50cm/s maximum velocity.
There is too much space under the baseline for the flow to be displayed. The baseline dropped for proper placement of the
waveforms in the display area.

column1:
Which of the following can be used to eliminate aliasing seen on a CW Doppler tracing?

A: decrease probe frequency
B: increase the scale
C: adjust baseline, increase scale and decrease probe frequency
D: none of the above
column2:
Answer: D. none of the above

Feedback:
Aliasing does not occur with a CW probe. The beam is not pulsed, so it is not limited by the pulse repetition frequency/Nyquist
limit.

column1:
Which of the following correctly describes the positive predictive value (PPV) of a testing technique?

A: If the test result is negative, PPV is the probability that the patient does not have disease
B: The ability of a test to detect disease when it IS present.
C: The ability of a test to rule out disease when it IS NOT present.
D: If the test result is positive, PPV is the probability that the patient actually has the disease
column2:
Answer: D. If the test result is positive, PPV is the probability that the patient actually has the disease

Feedback:
Positive Predictive Value-Calculates how often a positive study is correctly diagnosed; If the test result is positive, PPV is the
probability that the patient actually has the disease

column1:
What is the proper procedure for removing soiled gloves?

A: Pull the first glove off using the fingertip area, while keeping the dirty side out, and dispose of it. Repeat the process for the second glove.
B: Pull the outer cuff of the first glove toward the fingers while turning it inside out. Place the removed glove in the contralateral gloved hand and pull the outer cuff of the second glove toward the fingers while turning it inside out and enclosing the first glove.
C: Using sterile scissors, cut the glove across the back of the hand, peel it off and dispose. Repeat the process for the second glove.
D: Using sterile scissors, cut the glove across the palm of the hand, peel it off and dispose. Repeat the process for the second glove.
column2:
Answer: B. Pull the outer cuff of the first glove toward the fingers while turning it inside out. Place the removed glove in the contralateral gloved hand and pull the outer cuff of the second glove toward the fingers while turning it inside out and enclosing the first glove.

Feedback:
When removing used/soiled gloves, proper disposal is important for infection control. Pull the outer cuff of the first glove
toward the fingers while turning it inside out. Place the removed glove in the contralateral gloved hand and pull the outer cuff of
the second glove toward the fingers while turning it inside out and enclosing the first glove. The end result should be a "ball" of
gloves with the 1st glove trapped inside the second and the inside of the gloves exposed (not the outside) when disposing of
them.

column1:
The arrow indicates what ultrasound phenomenon?

A: Clutter
B: Blood flow within the Vasa vasorum
C: Cardiac pulsatility
D: String sign
column2:
Answer: A. Clutter

Feedback:
Color flow is demonstrated outside the vessel wall due to increased gain settings and decreased filter settings. The machine is
detecting low amplitude reflections from the vessel wall motion caused by blood flow within the lumen. By adjusting the gain,
filter and even the velocity scale, this issue can be corrected.

column1:
What should be done to adjust and improve the color Doppler displayed on the image?

A: Increase PRF
B: Increase velocity scale
C: Steer box
D: Decrease sample volume
column2:
Answer: D. Decrease sample volume

Feedback:
Color Doppler processing ability improves as the size of the area evaluated is decreased. The smaller sample size allows faster
frame rates and blood flow display. Increasing color velocity and PRF would decrease the amount of color currently displayed.

column1:
Which of the following will lead to more color displayed in real time in the abdominal aorta?

A: decrease color threshold
B: increase persistence
C: decrease color gain
D: increase sample size
column2:
Answer: B. increase persistence

Feedback:
Persistence settings determine how much of the previous image data is carried over into the current image frame. Increasing
this setting makes the live image appear smoother and with improved color display.

column1:
According to the Appropriate Use Criteria, which of the following is an appropriate indication for a screening ankle-brachial index exam?

A: Nocturnal pain or cramping with normal pulses on clinical exam
B: Diabetic neuropathy with normal pulses
C: Patient with low Framingham risk score
D: 58yr old male with diabetes
column2:
Answer: D. 58yr old male with diabetes

Feedback:
Dedicated ABI exams are recommended as a screening tool in patients with a femoral bruit or diminished pulses, patients
>50yrs with diabetes or smoking history and all patients over 70yrs of age..

column1:
By dividing the pulsatility index proximal to a stenosis by the pulsatility index from distal to a stenosis, you can evaluate the:

A: intraluminal diameter
B: intraluminal area
C: Reynolds number
D: damping factor
column2:
Answer: D. damping factor

Feedback:
The damping factor describes the degree of attenuation of the Doppler signal caused by the stenosis. The PI will decrease distal
to the stenosis. The greater the stenosis, the lower the distal PI and greater the damping factor.

column1:
Overgaining on an US exam can cause ________________________.

A: overestimation of Doppler velocity
B: increased posterior shadowing
C: underestimation of arterial stenosis
D: blood to appear anechoic
column2:
Answer: A. overestimation of Doppler velocity

Feedback:
Overgaining on a Doppler exam will cause a false increase is the peak velocity of the flow.

column1:
What Doppler artifact is demonstrated on the tracing?

A: Aliasing
B: Ghosting
C: Clutter
D: Cross talk
column2:
Answer: D. Cross talk

Feedback:
Cross Talk is a type of mirror imaging. It is caused by adjacent highly reflective structures, receiver gain too high or incident
Doppler angle near 90°.

column1:
What is the best way to adjust the console controls to eliminate the Doppler artifact?

A: increase the PW Doppler scale
B: increase the sweep speed
C: raise the baseline
D: turn down the Doppler gain
column2:
Answer: C. raise the baseline

Feedback:
The current settings have the baseline at 0 at the very bottom of the screen. Extremity arterial flow normally demonstrates
antegrade and retrograde flow. The baseline should be raised to demonstrate the flow moving in each direction.

column1:
Which of the following correctly describes the negative predictive value (NPV) of a testing technique?

A: The ability of a test to rule out disease when it IS NOT present.
B: The ability of a test to detect disease when it IS present.
C: If the test result is positive, NPV is the probability that the patient actually has the disease
D: If the test result is negative, NPV is the probability that the patient does not have disease
column2:
Answer: D. If the test result is negative, NPV is the probability that the patient does not have disease

Feedback:
Negative Predictive Value-Calculates how often a negative study is correctly diagnosed; If the test result is negative NPV is the
probability that the patient does not have disease.

column1:
Which of the following imaging parameters is responsible for the ability to resolve two atheromas that are side by side in an artery?

A: lateral resolution
B: axial resolution
C: dynamic range
D: contrast resolution
column2:
Answer: A. lateral resolution

Feedback:
Lateral resolution allows for the differentiation of objects that are perpendicular to the beam. Lateral resolution allows for the
differentiation of objects that are side by side.

column1:
If a renal artery Doppler exam demonstrates normal flow, but stenosis is identified on angiography evaluation, the US results are described as:

A: true negative
B: true positive
C: false negative
D: false positive
column2:
Answer: C. false negative

Feedback:
If the current exam is negative but the gold standard is positive = false negative results on current exam
If the current exam is positive but the gold standard is negative= false positive results on current exam
If the current exam is negative and the gold standard is negative = true negative results on current exam
If the current exam is positive and the gold standard is positive = true positive results on current exam

column1:
Which of the following describes why there is no aliasing on CW Doppler?

A: CW probes are always lower frequency than PW probes
B: the PW transducer uses a single element and the CW transducer uses two elements
C: CW Doppler does not require a sample volume to produce a Doppler waveform
D: CW Doppler does not require beam steering to produce a Doppler waveform
column2:
Answer: B. the PW transducer uses a single element and the CW transducer uses two elements

Feedback:
CW Doppler uses separate elements for sending and receiving the sound waves. This allows for Doppler evaluation without
depth limitations.

column1:
Which modality is preferred to diagnose fibromuscular dysplasia in the circle of Willis?

A: Transcranial Doppler
B: MRI
C: CTA
D: Angiography
column2:
Answer: D. Angiography

Feedback:
Cerebral angiography provides the best detail for the lumen characteristics of the intracerebral arteries.

column1:
A proximal femoral artery stenosis will cause the __________________ in the mid femoral artery.

A: Reynold's number to decrease
B: velocity to increase
C: acceleration time to decrease
D: acceleration time to increase
column2:
Answer: D. acceleration time to increase

Feedback:
Acceleration time refers to the time from the start of systole to peak systole. High resistance vessels have short acceleration
times. Low resistance vessels and areas distal to a stenosis will demonstrate longer acceleration times.

column1:
Which modality is preferred to diagnose an aortic dissection?

A: Duplex ultrasound
B: CTA
C: Angiography
D: MRI
column2:
Answer: B. CTA

Feedback:
CT scan is preferred for evaluation of AAA and dissection. The exam provides the best detail for the evaluation of aortic size and
lumen characteristics. All portions of the aorta can be seen with CT. Ultrasound can be limited by bony anatomy.

column1:
What blood flow characteristic is being measured on the Doppler tracings provided?

A: Resistive index
B: Pulsatility index
C: Acceleration time
D: Peak systolic velocity
column2:
Answer: C. Acceleration time

Feedback:
Acceleration time refers to the time from the onset of systole to the peak systolic velocity. The higher the resistance, the shorter
the AT.

column1:
Which of the following correctly describes a gold standard test?

A: A test that is preferred by most doctors to make a specific diagnosis
B: A type of test that has been used for over 15 years to successfully diagnose disease when it is present
C: A type of test that has been used for over 15 years to successfully diagnose rule out disease when it is absent
D: An accepted test that is assumed to be able to determine the true disease state of a patient, regardless of positive or negative test findings
column2:
Answer: D. An accepted test that is assumed to be able to determine the true disease state of a patient, regardless of positive or negative test findings

Feedback:
Gold Standard Testing refers to an accepted test that is assumed to be able to determine the true disease state of a patient,
regardless of positive or negative test findings or sensitivities or specificities of other diagnostic tests used. As new diagnostic
methods become available, the "gold standard" test may change over time. For example, an aortogram used to be the gold
standard test for the diagnosis of aortic dissection, but today MRA has become the new "gold standard" test for aortic dissection
because it has the highest sensitivity and specificity levels.

column1:
The ability of PW Doppler to determine the _____________________________ is the primary advantage over CW Doppler techniques.

A: location of stenosis in the vessel
B: velocity of flow
C: pulsatility of flow
D: direction of flow
column2:
Answer: A. location of stenosis in the vessel

Feedback:
CW Doppler provides no depth resolution during Doppler evaluation. CW Doppler is preferred for higher velocities and deeper
flow evaluations. PW Doppler is able to provide velocity, pulsatility and location of a stenosis.

column1:
________________________ aids in measurement of the acceleration time of the renal arteries.

A: Using a lower frequency transducer
B: Reducing rejection
C: Increasing the PRF
D: Increasing the sweep speed
column2:
Answer: D. Increasing the sweep speed

Feedback:
Faster sweep speeds produce wider waveforms on the display to facilitate easier and more accurate measurement of the AT.

column1:
What information from an angiography report can be correlated with the ultrasound results?

A: areas of turbulent flow
B: flow velocity
C: diameter stenosis
D: area stenosis
column2:
Answer: C. diameter stenosis

Feedback:
Angiography provides information on diameter stenosis of an artery because it produces a 2-dimensional longitudinal image of
vessels. Area stenosis cannot be evaluated without a transverse cut of the vessels. It is limited in that it can only estimate
diameter stenosis (no area stenosis), does not offer information on flow velocity, and cannot demonstrate areas of turbulent
flow

column1:
The image displayed is from the PTA from an arterial Duplex exam with normal ABIs and minimal atherosclerosis. Which of the following explains the findings?

A: Medial calcification is causing increased resistance to the flow causing a peaked appearance of the monophasic waveform.
B: The Doppler gain is too low and pertinent information is not displayed.
C: The wall filter is set too high and is removing pertinent information from the display.
D: The waveform is normal and is consistent with the other findings of the exam.
column2:
Answer: C. The wall filter is set too high and is removing pertinent information from the display.

Feedback:
Anytime a peaked monophasic waveform is displayed, adjustments to the machine settings should be made to better evaluate
the flow for the diastolic flow components. Decrease the scale, adjust the baseline, decrease the rejection level or filter and
readjust probe.

column1:
Increasing the spectral Doppler sample size will increase the ___________________.

A: Doppler shift
B: size of the spectral window
C: spectral broadening
D: sweep speed
column2:
Answer: C. spectral broadening

Feedback:
Smaller Doppler sample volumes will produce a waveform with a larger spectral window because the smaller volume of cells
sampled are moving at similar speeds. Larger Doppler sample volumes will produce a waveform with a small or absent spectral
window because the larger volume of cells sampled are moving at varied speeds.

column1:
When the velocity of flow is calculated for vessels evaluated on a TCD exam, a ____________ degree angle of incidence is assumed.

A: 60
B: 0
C: 45
D: 30
column2:
Answer: B. 0

Feedback:
When the velocity of flow is calculated for vessels evaluated on a TCD exam, a 0-degree angle of incidence is assumed.

column1:
Which of the following is a complication of series stenosis in an artery?

A: velocity and stenosis can be underestimated at the second stenosis
B: velocity and stenosis can be overestimated at the second stenosis
C: velocity and stenosis can be overestimated at the first stenosis
D: velocity and stenosis can be underestimated at the first stenosis
column2:
Answer: A. velocity and stenosis can be underestimated at the second stenosis

Feedback:
Stenoses in a series - multiple areas of stenosis in the same vessel will increase the resistance to flow more than a single
stenotic area; velocity and stenosis can be underestimated at the second stenosis because of the drop in velocity after the first
stenosis
Stenoses in parallel - stenosis in different vessels coursing in the same direction (collaterals); has a lesser effect on resistance to
flow than series stenoses
